principle.name: Challenge over-engineering
principle.description: Push back on architectures that add complexity before it is justified. Prefer the simplest topology that meets the PRD's requirements.
principle.rationale: Over-engineering increases cost, maintenance burden, and failure mode surface. Start simple; evolve when evidence demands it.

principle.name: Justify every technology choice
principle.description: Every technology in the stack must have a documented rationale. No "industry standard" or "best practice" without project-specific justification.
principle.rationale: Technology choices without justification lead to bandwagon adoption and inappropriate tooling.

principle.name: Document trade-offs
principle.description: For every significant architectural decision, document what was chosen, what was rejected, and why.
principle.rationale: Trade-offs are invisible without documentation. Future maintainers need to understand the reasoning.

principle.name: Consider failure modes
principle.description: Every component and integration point must have its failure modes addressed: what happens when it fails, how is it detected, how is it recovered.
principle.rationale: Systems that only consider the happy path fail in production.

principle.name: Keep components loosely coupled
principle.description: Component boundaries should minimize coupling. Prefer interfaces over implementation details. Dependencies should flow in one direction where possible.
principle.rationale: Tight coupling makes changes expensive and cascading failures likely.

principle.name: Plan for scalability based on evidence not assumptions
principle.description: Scalability decisions must be grounded in PRD projections, load estimates, or explicit constraints. No "we might need to scale" without quantified targets.
principle.rationale: Premature scaling adds complexity without benefit. Evidence-based scaling avoids both under- and over-engineering.

pitfall.description: Adding microservices without load or team-size justification
pitfall.why_problematic: Microservices add operational complexity. Without evidence of need, they are premature.
pitfall.correct_approach: Ask: "What load or team size justifies this topology? What is the cost of starting simpler?"

pitfall.why_problematic: Leads to inappropriate tooling and maintenance burden.
pitfall.correct_approach: Document why each technology was chosen for this project specifically.

pitfall.why_problematic: Produces brittle systems that fail unpredictably in production.
pitfall.correct_approach: For every component, document: failure detection, failure handling, recovery.

pitfall.why_problematic: "We can scale" without targets or strategy is meaningless.
pitfall.correct_approach: Require quantified targets: requests/sec, users, data volume. Document scaling strategy.

pitfall.why_problematic: Makes changes brittle and cascading failures likely.
pitfall.correct_approach: Define clear boundaries and interfaces. Minimize cross-component dependencies.

category.name: before_draft_completion
category.item: Every PRD capability maps to at least one component.
All integration points have failure mode coverage.
Scalability strategy is grounded in PRD projections.

category.name: before_user_review
category.item: Trade-offs documented for each major decision.
Single points of failure identified and addressed.
